title: chat_using() uses OnceLock to allocate the capability URN vec exactly once
description: chat_using() returns a &'static [String] backed by a OnceLock<Vec<String>>. A contributor might replace this with a function that allocates a new Vec<String> on every call, arguing that two allocations per request is negligible. The OnceLock approach is correct because: (1) the String values are constants — allocating them repeatedly would be noise in a profiler trace; (2) returning a &'static slice lets build_request call .to_vec() once per request rather than constructing strings every time. The pattern is idiomatic for &'static data that cannot be expressed as a literal.
